_provenance: hand-derived; return series are synthetic; Pearson correlations computed independently in the derivation note below
_derivation: series_a = [0.01, 0.02, -0.01, 0.03, -0.02]; series_b = [0.01, 0.02, -0.01, 0.03, -0.02] (identical copy of a). Pearson r(a,b) = 1.0 exactly. n_obs = 5. series_c = [-0.01, -0.02, 0.01, -0.03, 0.02] (perfect inverse of a). Pearson r(a,c) = -1.0 exactly. r(b,c) = -1.0 exactly. All computed with population-corrected sample Pearson (ddof=1 implicit in the formula; Pearson r is scale-invariant so ddof choice does not affect the result).
_comment: n_obs=5 is below thin-data threshold (30). Correlations are well-defined but flagged as thin.

expected_pairs._comment: Perfect positive correlation; n_obs=5 < 30 so thin_data=true
Also -1.0 because b is identical to a and c is the inverse

_tolerance_rationale: Exact algebraic result for these synthetic series; only floating-point rounding contributes error; 1e-9 captures that while remaining tighter than any implementation bug
